name: Suma
summary: It should be framed conservatively because traditional-use breadth exceeds evidence depth.
description: It should be framed conservatively because traditional-use breadth exceeds evidence depth.
contraindications: hormone replacement therapy or hormonal contraceptives or other hormone-altering medication because suma has been shown to raise estradiol and testosterone and progesterone levels in research
estrogen-sensitive or hormone-sensitive cancer such as breast or prostate cancer without clinician guidance due to suma's documented hormonal activity
pregnancy or breastfeeding without clinician guidance due to insufficient safety data

sources.title: Antiproliferative assay of suma or Brazilian ginseng (Hebanthe eriantha) methanolic extract on HCT116 and 4T1 cancer cell lines, in vitro toxicity on Artemia salina larvae, and antibacterial activity
Selected herbals and human exercise performance

dosage: Preparation-specific; use standardized product label for Preparation varies by tradition and product form and require source-backed dosage review before therapeutic claims.
